ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #5, REPORT, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jan-03-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Lee, for petitioner Cristina Chavez.

The Court noted that its concern was with the trip to Mexico by the Petitioner. Mr. Lee acknowledged that as the Court had denied the petition to bring the Mexican property into the estate through ancillary proceedings, that property was not part of the estate. He stated that the petitioner believed that having the property in the estate would benefit the heirs of the estate, and that the trip was a reasonable expense by the petitioner. He stated that she has waived fees for administration of the estate, and is only seeking the reimburseent of her costs. He said that while the receipt submitted to the Court showed airfare for the petitioner, her mother, and two of the heirs, who had all travelled to Mexico together, the petitioner was only requesting reimbursement of her individual fare. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Anasse. (Not reported)

Petition For Approval Of Second Amended Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Aug-15-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Lee for Cristina Chavez; Trisha Friedeberg, Guardian ad litem for Levity Chavez; Lawrence Siracusa for Rudolpho Chavez; Nathan James for creditor Nelsy Flores.

The Court noted that under the settlement agreement, the interest of Rudolfo Chavez in the real property is being bought out, but not the interest of Levity Chavez. He asked why this would be in Levity's best interest. Ms. Friedeberg responded that Levity is currently living in Mexico with her mother, and cannot return to the United States at this time. She stated that Levity desires to maintain her residence in Mexico with her mother. Ms. Friedeberg explained that under the terms of the settlement agreement, Levity and her mother will be allowed to live in their current home in Mexico rent-free at least until Levity reaches the age of 18. If at that time she desires to return to the United States, she will still have an interest in the property located here. She added that if her interest in the property were sold now and the proceeds were placed in a guardianship account, it would be unlikely to generate interest in an equivalent amount to the appreciation ov value for San Francisco real estate. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Nuque. (Not reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-18-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Lee for (administrator) Cristina Chavez. Andrew Schultz specially appearing for court appointed counsel Lawrence Siracusa for Rodolfo Chavez. Nathan James for and with (creditor) Nelsy Flores.

The Court stated that this is no longer a final accounting, that it is to approve the first 3 accountings and to proceed with the other issues raised here. Mr. Lee stated that he spoke with Mr. Siracusa regarding the idea of refinancing the properties and getting an appraisal enabling the estate to pay off Rudy's share and the mothers claim to be made, that there are still some issues with the tenants, that they will put together a pre-approval of the loan, that the agreement is to hold on to the properties, that they are looking for a way to cash Rudy out and trying to pay the claim. The Court asked if there were any objections to a continuance. Mr. James stated that they do not know what a continuance will do, that Mr. Lee has known of their position to sell the property and that there is a 24 year old son living there who is not paying rent. The Court stated that there is enough of a feeling that it may work to try it. Mr. James stated that he spoke with Mr. Siracusa who is on the same page regarding selling the properties, that the properties are no longer under water, that it would behoove his client and her son to be cashed out at fair market value instead of an appraisal. Mr. Schultz stated that it is a concern that the minor receives a full share and that they are inclined to prefer to list and sell the properties. The Court stated that all it has before it are non final accountings, that there are no objections to the accountings themselves, that it will approve the accounting and set a status for the presentation of a plan going forward, that it should be a viable plan that would avoid a sale and fairly treat all interests or a plan to sell the property. Mr. Lee requested 2 months and stated that it should not take long to put together an agreeable procedure. The Court granted the accounting petition (subject to review of the order) and instructed Mr. Lee to submit a revised order. Status on the administrator's plan for closing the estate was set for May 3, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)
